steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: It is generally true that technological change is a linear process, in which once-useful technologies are replaced by new and better ones is an independent clause.

After the blank: the reawakening of interest in the steam engine (from advocates of carbon-neutral rail travel) reminds us that ostensibly obsolete technologies may be brought back into service to address society's changing needs is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for semicolons with conjunctive adverbs
steps.content:
A semicolon before a conjunctive adverb and a comma after it join two independent clauses; choice C correctly uses this pattern with “ones; even so,”.

Choices A, B, and D violate this rule by placing the semicolon after the adverb (A), omitting it entirely (B), or using only commas (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
